title: Help
heading: How OpenFarmPlanner works
intro: OpenFarmPlanner consists of several pages that work together:

sections.title: Growing areas
points: Create locations, fields, and beds here.

sections.title: Cultures
points: Enter your crop data here.

sections.title: Planting plans
points: Plan which crop is grown in which bed here.

sections.title: Planting calendar
points: See the timeline for your planting plans here.

sections.title: Seed demand
points: This calculates how much seed you need.

workflow: Growing areas → Cultures → Planting plans → Planting calendar → Seed demand

dashboard.title: Help: Overview
dashboard.intro: The overview shows the current state of your project and gives you quick access to the main areas.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Use the navigation to edit data or open planning pages.
Use quick actions for frequent tasks.

locations.title: Help: Locations
locations.intro: Manage the locations in your project here. They form the top level of your growing area structure.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Create locations and add coordinates, address, soil type, or notes.
Edit existing locations from the list actions.

sections.title: How this connects to other pages
points: On the growing areas page, you add fields and beds for each location.
Tasks are derived from planting plans.

fields.title: Help: Fields
fields.intro: Manage fields within a location here. A field groups multiple beds.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Use “New” to create a field.
Click table rows to edit entries.

beds.title: Help: Beds
beds.intro: Manage your beds here. Each bed belongs to a field and can later be used in planting plans.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Use “New” to create a bed.
Click table rows to edit entries.

sections.title: How this connects to other pages
points: In planting plans, you assign crops to specific beds.

areas.title: Help: Growing areas - list view
areas.intro: Manage your growing area structure from location to bed here. The hierarchical list is useful for creating and editing areas.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Click a cell to edit entries directly.
Click table headers to sort or filter.

sections.title: How this connects to other pages
points: The area structure is the basis for your planning.
In planting plans, you assign crops to individual beds.
The graphical view shows the same areas visually.

symbols.createPlan: Create a planting plan for this bed
symbols.dimensions: Set length and width orientation for the graphical view

cultures.title: Help: Cultures
cultures.intro: Manage crops and their key properties here. These data are used in planting plans, calendar, and seed demand.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Select a crop to view or edit details.
Create, export, delete, or use crops directly for a planting plan.
Use the public crop library to share or import crops.

sections.title: How this connects to other pages
points: In planting plans, you assign crops to specific beds and time periods.
The planting calendar and seed demand use these data for timing and quantity planning.

symbols.createPlan: Create a planting plan for this crop

plantingPlans.title: Help: Planting plans
plantingPlans.intro: Plan which crop is grown in which bed and when. This page connects growing areas with crop data and feeds the calendar and seed demand.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Create and edit entries directly in the table.
Use “New” to add another plan.
Changes to area, time period, or crop are applied directly.
On mobile, create entries with the floating plus button.

sections.title: How this connects to other pages
points: Planting plans use the area structure from growing areas.
The planting calendar shows the resulting timeline.
Seed demand is calculated from these plans.

symbols.mobileAdd: Quickly create a new entry on mobile
symbols.notes: Open notes and attachments for an entry
symbols.edit: Change values directly in the table

calendar.title: Help: Planting calendar
calendar.intro: The planting calendar shows the timeline of your planting plans. It helps you spot occupancy, propagation phases, and possible overlaps.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Use tabs to switch between occupancy and propagation views.
Show or hide the weekly yield calendar as needed.
Move through the timeline to review upcoming periods.
Hover entries to see details in a tooltip.

sections.title: How this connects to other pages
points: The calendar is based on planting plan data.
Changes to time periods in planting plans update this view.

symbols.switch: Show or hide the weekly yield calendar

seedDemand.title: Help: Seed demand
seedDemand.intro: See how much seed you need for your planned crops. Quantities are calculated from crop data, areas, and planting plan entries.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Choose a supplier per crop to receive package suggestions.
Review required quantities and suggested package combinations in the table.
Use the view to align orders with your plan.

sections.title: How this connects to other pages
points: The basis comes from planting plans and crop data.
Supplier and package information adds purchasing context.
Changes to crops or plans directly affect demand.

suppliers.title: Help: Suppliers
suppliers.intro: Manage seed suppliers and sources here. These data enrich crops and help with later seed planning.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Use “New” to add a supplier.
Edit existing entries directly in the list.
Remove suppliers you no longer need.

sections.title: How this connects to other pages
points: Suppliers add purchasing information to crops.
In seed demand, they help select matching packages and sources.

graphical.title: Help: Growing areas - graphical view
graphical.intro: See locations, fields, and beds arranged spatially here. The graphical view helps you review and adjust the area structure visually.

sections.title: How to use it
points: Click a bed or field to show details.
Move elements in “Edit” mode.
Drag empty space to pan the view.
Zoom with the mouse wheel or a pinch gesture.

sections.title: How this connects to other pages
points: The graphical view shows the same areas as the list view.

symbols.panArrows: Move view up, left, right, or down
